We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior IT Support Technician
description: **The Role.** We are looking for an IT Support Technician to join our internal IT team and provide first\-line support to staff across multiple offices throughout Ireland. This is a great opportunity for someone with around 1\-2 years' IT support experience who is looking to build their career in a busy, hands\-on environment. The role is focused on day\-to\-day user support, responding to tickets, troubleshooting common IT issues, setting up devices, and helping staff both remotely and on site. You will work closely with the wider IT team and play an important role in keeping users productive and IT services running smoothly. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** * Provide first\-line IT support to staff across multiple office locations * Log, triage, and manage incidents and service requests through the IT ticketing system * Support users by email, phone, remote support tools, and in person * Troubleshoot day\-to\-day issues relating to laptops, desktops, printers, mobile phones, tablets, and other end\-user devices * Set up, deploy, and maintain user devices and equipment * Assist with user account administration and general Microsoft 365 / Windows support tasks * Maintain accurate device and asset records * Escalate more complex issues to senior members of the IT team where required * Assist with routine IT maintenance tasks, software updates, and general housekeeping * Support small IT projects and office visits as needed **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Approximately 1\-2 years' experience in an IT support or service desk role * Good working knowledge of Windows and Microsoft Office / Microsoft 365 * A solid understanding of core IT fundamentals is essential, including IP addressing, DNS and DHCP. Candidates should understand how these technologies enable devices and systems to communicate across a network. * Experience supporting end users with common hardware and software issues * Strong communication skills and a helpful, professional approach * Ability to manage and prioritise workload through a ticketing system * A genuine interest in IT and a willingness to learn and develop * Ability to work well as part of a team and use initiative when required * Full driving licence and own transport, as occasional travel to other office locations may be required. (Travel expenses will be paid per a kilometre travelled) **Desirable** * Exposure to Active Directory, Office 365 administration, basic networking, and remote support tools * Experience with device builds, patching, or asset management * IT certifications such as CompTIA A\+, Network\+, CCNA or Microsoft certifications **What The Role Offers** * A hands\-on opportunity to build broad IT support experience * Exposure to a wide range of technologies and day\-to\-day operational support * The chance to develop your skills within a busy internal IT team * Ongoing learning and practical experience across multiple office environments * A strong culture of continuous learning, with encouragement for team members to pursue relevant certifications and further develop their technical skills **As occasional travel to company locations is required, candidates must hold a full clean driving licence valid for use in Ireland, or be willing to obtain one within the first 6\-9 months of employment.** **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior QS
description: *Please note: This job requires you to have Irish/UK experience and can not offer any sponsorship* **Job Summary:** Our client is offering an excellent opportunity for a Quantity Surveyor to join a growing business with a clear strategic plan for expansion. Reporting to the Commercial Director and based in East Meath on a hybrid basis, this role will suit someone looking to progress their career in a company with strong long\-term ambition. **Role Description:** * Manage all aspects of quantity surveying for construction projects. * Develop the supply chain. * Oversee project procurement. * Prepare monthly project payment applications and cost reports. * Monitor project costs and agree variations. * Remeasure site works. * Report monthly to senior management on financial performance. * Prepare and present cost and risk analysis. * Manage all aspects of tendering, including financial assessments. * Ensure best value is achieved across all elements. * Audit and report on subcontractor and supplier returns. **Suitable Candidates Should Have:** * Residential experience, with modular or timber frame experience preferred. * A minimum of 2 years’ experience in a similar role. * A third\-level qualification in Construction, Building, or a related field. * Strong communication skills and in\-depth knowledge of construction practices, trades, and techniques. * A proven track record in securing profitable projects. * Excellent IT skills. * A full clean European driving licence and access to transport. * Strong Microsoft Office skills. * The ability to work well as part of a team. * The ability to meet deadlines. * Excellent numeracy and computer skills. * A professional presence, dress, and demeanour. **Salary:** Negotiable, and will include a suitable benefits package.

title: Junior Transaction Reporting Analyst
description: **Role Overview:** The Junior Transaction Reporting Analyst will support the firm’s daily transaction and trade reporting obligations, ensuring timely, accurate, and compliant submissions to regulators while adhering to the relevant controls. The role also offers exposure to regulatory change, process improvement, and broader reporting initiatives, providing a strong foundation for development within regulatory reporting. As you progress in the role, you will contribute to supporting the Co\-Heads of Regulatory Reporting in the ongoing enhancement of the firm’s transaction reporting framework, support the assessment and implementation of new regulatory requirements, and assist with wider regulatory and business projects across the firm. **About the Team:** The Transaction Reporting Team is responsible for the completion and monitoring of several transaction reporting obligations to the Central Bank of Ireland and to the relevant Trade Repositories. These include MiFID Transaction Reporting, MiFID Post Trade Transparency, EMIR Data Reporting and SFTR Transaction Reporting. The team is also involved in the firm Shareholding Disclosure Obligations (including substantial shareholding rules, takeover disclosure and short selling), and in other regulatory obligations (i.e. monthly metrics). **Main Responsibilities:** * Prepare and submit transaction and trade reports to the relevant National Competent Authorities within required timelines * Investigate trading activities and identify reportable transactions. * Perform and document reporting controls to ensure data accuracy and completeness. * Reconcile and compare data sets between Trade Repository and internal systems, identifying mismatches and proactively solving the breaks. * Develop relationships with internal stakeholders, including Front Office, Compliance, Operations and NBC Group teams to adapt reporting solutions with the evolving nature of the business. **Skills and Experience Required:** * Advanced Microsoft Excel and VBA skills. * Degree in Finance, Accounting, Business Administration or Law * Minimum 1 years’ experience in the financial services industry; regulatory or reporting exposure is beneficial * Previous experience with transaction reporting (MiFIR, EMIR and/or SFTR), across asset classes (equity, credit, foreign exchange) centrally cleared and OTC is advantageous. * Ability to work independently, use initiative and perform well under pressure to meet deadlines * Experience in the independent assessment of new Regulations to ensure compliance with the firm’s business practices and experience of adapting the reporting framework to new trading activities. **About Us:** NBCGF is wholly owned direct subsidiary of National Bank of Canada (“NBC”). The main strategic purpose of NBCGF is to enable the NBC Group (“NBC Group,” meaning National Bank of Canada and all its subsidiaries) to grow and diversify its presence in Europe while allowing for a natural development of its existing business relationships and the establishment of new ones in the European markets. NBC has over 150 years of history and is currently the 6th largest Canadian bank. Through its branch and subsidiary network, NBC can service clients throughout North America and Europe. NBC’s strategy is to further develop NBCGF as its hub for trading and financial services in Europe. **What NBCGF has to Offer:** Access to competitive working conditions, a wide range of employee benefits, and a dynamic work environment. Open, respectful workplace where all employees can achieve their full potential. NBCGF recognises that the culture of an organisation and the performance of its workforce are not mutually exclusive. Since our inception in 2013, NBCGF has worked tirelessly to ensure our cultural framework aligns not only with the Code of Conduct and Ethics of our parent company, NBC, but with our own environmental and local values. Promoting and supporting diversity in the workplace is an important aspect of good people’s management\- it’s about valuing everyone in the organisation as an individual. We at NBCGF have an effective diversity and inclusion approach concerning our employees. There are no silos in our teams and management strive to add value to our organisation, by contributing to employee well\-being and engagement. NBCGF takes pride in its diverse work force, employing and cultivating professionals from all over the globe. Working at NBCGF can take different forms and it is built around the relationship of trust between the employer and its employees. NBCGF operates a hybrid working model where the employee’s time can be split between the office and a remote location on a schedule which may vary. The model considers customer expectations, organisational needs, and constraints, as well as the employees’ need for flexibility and the nature of the work they perform. What is important is having the right arrangements in place so that you can best perform your role. We're putting people first. We're a bank on a human scale that stands out for its courage, entrepreneurial culture, and passion for people. Our mission is to have a positive impact on people’s lives. We welcome all candidates! What can you bring to our team?

title: Keyholder (Full time) - Kildare Village
description: ### **ABOUT THE POSITION** With over 430 retail stores worldwide, our associates are essential to our business. From ensuring product availability on the sales floor to creating eye\-catching visual displays to addressing customer inquiries to running an efficient check out process, our associates create memorable customer experiences while simultaneously assisting the Store Leadership team support Columbia Sportswear Company’s mission of “Connecting Active People with Their Passions.” As a Retail Associate II you will serve as a brand ambassador to provide exceptional customer service; contributing to the profitability and customer experience in the store. Within this role you will employ your natural problem solving and sales abilities to answer customer questions and make compelling product recommendations while supporting Columbia Sportswear Companies values to enhance customer service and teamwork. The Retail Associate II role is only used for Retail Associates who are in the Designated Key Holder Program. This role is based at our outlet store in **Kildare, Ireland** . Applicants must have the legal right to work in Ireland ( **a valid work permit is required** ). ### **HOW YOU’LL MAKE A DIFFERENCE** * Provide exceptional customer service by responding to consumer questions or issues regarding product features, benefits and availability; engage with consumers to understand their needs and preferences (e.g., demonstrate and explain merchandise, make suggestions on suitable options for consumers etc.). * Maintain store standards for visual merchandising, cleanliness, safety; retrieves merchandise from sales floor, stock room, or other inventory locations. * Perform all required cash register transactions (e.g., sales, returns, exchanges, connecting consumer to loyalty program) efficiently and accurately. * Contribute to store profitability by receiving, handling, replenishing and processing incoming and outgoing product flow in the stores accurately and efficiently * Perform designated keyholder duties as needed which may include opening and closing store duties. ### **YOU HAVE** * No specific education required (High School Diploma or GED preferred) * 2 (or less) years of experience in position or specialization * Skill operating a Point of Sales (POS) system, other in\-store computerized systems, and telephone ### **JOB CONDITIONS** * Frequently in a more active environment (i.e., requires ability to constantly move about) that can be physical or strenuous in nature (frequently adjusting/moving items weighing up to 40 lbs.), may frequently use specialized equipment, may have exposure to dust and noise * Occasionally requires the ability to work in place * Ability to clearly communicate with others * Availability to work a flexible retail schedule, which may change seasonally and in response to location\-specific business trends or forecasts. All local, state, and federal laws are adhered to when evaluating your availability. *This job description is not meant to be an all\-inclusive list of duties and responsibilities, but constitutes a general definition of the position's scope and function in the company.*

title: Knowledge Lawyer (Finance)
description: We are a leading international law and professional services firm providing legal, corporate and fiduciary services to global corporations, financial institutions, capital market participants and investment fund managers. With a global presence spanning the Americas, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, we advise on the laws of Bermuda, the British Virgin Islands, the Cayman Islands, Guernsey, Ireland and Jersey. We treat everyone as the intelligent professional they are. Our approach is to trust and empower our people to deliver consistently, and enable them to succeed. Diversity is our secret weapon – it’s the sheer breadth of Walkers people that makes us who we are – gathered from across the globe and fluent in languages, jurisdictions and cultures that help us to mirror our clients and keep our own thinking in tune with the world in which we operate. **The Role** Walkers is currently seeking to recruit an experienced and enthusiastic Knowledge Lawyer to join our highly regarded Finance and Capital Markets team in Dublin, on a permanent and full\-time basis (5 days). The option of a 4 day working week may be considered for the right candidate. Hybrid working options and flexible hours available. This is an exciting new role in our market\-leading and award\-winning structured finance \& securitisation practice. The successful candidate will work closely with the partners in the Irish office to help ensure that the team's knowledge and development objectives are achieved. We advise clients on the full range of banking, debt capital markets, finance, private credit, fund finance, real estate finance, securitisation and structured credit transactions. Your work will be interesting, rewarding, challenging and impactful. This is an excellent opportunity to work in an internationally focused, professional organisation which places huge emphasis on continuous personal professional development, mentoring and career development within a collegiate team\-based environment. The successful candidate will join our existing KM team and will support the strategies and processes to develop and maintain legal and professional resources including: * precedents; * know\-how; * client\-facing guides; * practice notes; * management of knowledge dissemination to Walkers lawyers; and * familiarity with Walkers external resources (for example library, journal subscriptions and online resources), In addition, they will also have the opportunity to support the Legal and Technical Training Manager in the development and delivery of training to the fee earners to enable them to effectively meet the needs of the clients in an evolving legal and regulatory landscape. This role offers the opportunity to drive excellence within the practice group while working closely with partners and senior knowledge lawyers to deliver high\-impact knowledge management and training for a high\-performing, dynamic and fast\-paced team. **Reporting** The role will report to the Co\-Heads of the FCM group. **Key Focus Areas** * Banking \& Finance * Debt Capital Markets * Derivatives \& risk transfer arrangements * Fund Finance * Green Finance and ESG products * Insurance\-Linked Securities * Private Credit * Real Estate Finance * Securitisation including ABS, CMBS, CLOs, RMBS \& synthetic securitisations * Securities law and regulatory considerations * Structured Credit * Structured Products * Company law **Duties \& Responsibilities** **Precedents and Know\-How** * Impact Assessments: monitoring and carrying out impact assessments on the implications of new legal and regulatory developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities for clients * Precedents \& Protocols: assisting with the maintenance and development of the Firm’s precedents, processes and knowledge systems, including automated documentation on DocumentDrafter, and providing related training and support where required * Current Awareness: management of timely knowledge dissemination to relevant fee earners * Horizon Scanning: reviewing existing, new and proposed legislation and case law, keeping abreast of developments in Irish \& EU legislation generally and disseminating the information thereon to relevant fee earners and other relevant practice groups **Training** * New Joiner Training: designing and maintaining suitable pre\-joining materials to be provided to new staff and assisting with the training of new fee earners in relation to the legal resources available, precedents, documentation standards \& protocols and other relevant knowledge materials * Legal Resources Training: train new lawyers, secretaries and corporate services executives on the availability of legal resources * Fee Earner Training: assisting with the coordination of a team\-wide training programme and assisting where possible with targeted training including the delivery of training sessions to fee earners on relevant aspects of Irish \& EU law and in relation to legal developments * Client Training: Preparing and delivering high\-quality briefing papers and presentations for clients on relevant legal and regulatory topics **Management and Administration** * Actively participating in the evaluation and implementation of legal technology solutions to improve knowledge management processes, internal protocols and to enhance research efficiency within the Firm * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with stakeholder practice groups and offices through a variety of channels including face\-to\-face meetings, regular group calls, attending wider practice group meetings, social events and incentive\-driven projects. * Support the development and/or reinforcement of relationships with other business services departments (for example, preparing first drafts of thought leadership pieces/directory entries with the marketing and business development teams). * Support as directed the activities of the various Precedents and Know\-How Committees and/or the Global Opinion Committee, as applicable, as well as other working groups and committees by specific request. **Business Development** * Assisting in the production and/or updating of relevant Guides, thought leadership pieces, articles and practice notes * Liaising with senior fee earners and the Business Development Group in relation to website updates, thought leadership pieces, legal journal submissions etc **Skills \& Personal Attributes** The successful candidate should be a qualified solicitor or barrister in Ireland, Northern Ireland or England \& Wales with at least 4\-5 years PQE, ideally with a leading Irish or international law firm or in independent practice in the Law Library, or equivalent, with experience in one or more of finance, capital markets, structured finance \& securitisation, regulatory \& compliance, or general corporate. Candidate should have a proven strong academic track record including at least a 2:1 honours degree in any discipline. We are looking for candidates who can demonstrate the following: **Essential** * collegiate and thoughtful team player; * strong academic and experience credentials; * excellent research, analytical, technical, drafting skills and attention to detail; * an in\-depth knowledge of the types of resources for which a Knowledge Lawyer is responsible (for example, library resources, journals and online resources) and strong understanding of the importance of effective knowledge management within a law firm, with a particular focus on the needs of a busy Finance department; * client focused and commercial aptitude with an interest in/experience of business development and growing a service offering; * a calm and measured approach to problem solving; * excellent oral and written communication skills and ability to tailor this to communicate with partners, colleagues and (where appropriate) with the Firm's clients; * ability to work under pressure and responsive to the expectations of partners and clients; * able to prioritise competing demands; * excellent time management, project management and organisational skills; and * participate in continuously improving services through critically evaluating systems of work, promoting new ideas and actively developing service improvements. **Desirable:** * experience in Banking Asset Finance or Finance \& Capital Markets desirable, but not essential * experience with traditional online resources including BetterRegulation, WestLaw IE, Practical Law (PLC), Bloomsbury, Lexis\+, LMA online etc; * experience with Harvey AI, Co\-Pilot and other legal AI tech; * strong experience with Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and MS Excel. * knowledge of SharePoint, document management and document automation software an advantage. **Benefits** We offer excellent progression opportunities along with a competitive remuneration package and benefits including: * Competitive Salary * Supportive, collegiate environment and open culture * Prominent international clients and challenging work * Working directly with partners * 'Dress for your day' dress code * Discretionary annual bonus scheme; * Generous annual leave * Agile Working Policy * Health insurance cover * Pension scheme and firm contribution * Life Assurance * Paid Maternity \& Paternity leave * Income Protection * Taxsaver and Bike to Work schemes * Professional Membership Fees paid * Learning \& Development and Mentorship programmes * Educational bursaries and assistance * Client secondment opportunities * Social Committee, DEI Committee, CSR Committee and various sports, social and charity initiatives * Subsidised gym access/fitness classes * Employee Assistance Programme * VDU eye testing and medical screening * Wellness programme Walkers global is an equal opportunity employer. Equality and diversity are key to our global identity and an integral part of our goal to continue being an employer of choice. We are committed to a work environment that supports all individuals irrespective of gender, ethnicity, nationality, race, religion, marital status, age, disability, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or any other applicable legally protected characteristics. We make every effort to ensure that employment opportunities are open and accessible to all purely on the basis of personal ability.

title: Legal Quality Analyst (compliance) I Dublin
description: **Description** **Quality Analyst \- Compliance Operations** **Type:** Permanent position **Shifts:** 5 days/week, Monday\-Friday 8am\-5pm (subject to change), 8 working hours with an hour lunch break Are you passionate about upholding the highest standards of quality and compliance? We are seeking a meticulous Quality Analyst to help maintain and enhance the integrity of our client's platform. In this role, you'll ensure our policies are followed across various compliance areas, from intellectual property to data privacy, specializing in one of our four compliance streams. **What We Offer** * Competitive Rewards: Monthly performance bonuses and employee referral program (refer a friend and earn up to €1,500\) * Comprehensive Benefits: Company pension, medical insurance contributions, and wellness programs * Career Development: Stable, permanent role with regular training and opportunities to advance your career on a global or local level within TELUS Digital * Inspiring Workspace: Vibrant office with relaxation zones and game areas (table tennis, foosball table) * Transport Perks: Parking, Bike to Work scheme, and TaxSaver Leap Cards * Work\-Life Balance: 20 vacation days and wellness programs * Community Impact: Be part of meaningful CSR (corporate social responsibility) projects and community initiatives * Global Exposure: 50\+ benefits and services to choose from in an international environment **Overview Of Tasks** As a Quality Analyst, you'll specialize in one of the following compliance streams: * Contractual Operations (COPS): Ensure our client meets licensing and regulatory obligations with a focus on the music industry. Review and act on potential licensing violations by removing or blocking content, and support global regulatory efforts to balance content access with licensing limits. * Scaled Regulatory Operations (SRO): Ensure our client's compliance with local laws and regulations. Design, build, and scale operational solutions to address the ever\-evolving regulatory landscape, mitigate risks associated with non\-compliance, and collaborate with internal stakeholders. * Privacy Operations: Support our client's platforms by ensuring user data privacy requests (data access, deletion, and portability) are correctly implemented, tested, and aligned with global privacy regulations including GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, LGPD, and other regional requirements. * Intellectual Property Operations: Independently investigate and validate cases across copyright, trademark, counterfeit, and brand rights protection workflows. Ensure adherence to global regulations including the Digital Services Act (DSA) and Digital Millennium Copyright Act (DMCA), identify policy gaps, perform root cause analysis, and recommend improvements to policies and processes. **We Are Looking For Someone Who Can** * Demonstrate a keen eye for catching non\-compliance and inconsistencies with strong attention to detail * Make quick, informed decisions based on guidelines and policies * Excel at managing workflows and maintaining a high level of organization * Articulate complex issues clearly and professionally with strong communication skills * Show passion for maintaining high standards of policy and legal adherence * Demonstrate C1 level English proficiency * Bring experience in a Compliance role (AML, KYC, etc.) or Policy Quality Analyst role * Utilize internal tools and external resources for verification of trademark registrations and validation of copyright claims * Provide regular feedback to QA team leads and policy experts * Collaborate with global teams to ensure consistent quality standards **Join our team and apply now!** Apply today and if you're successful with your initial application, our recruitment department will be in touch within 7 days. Are you worried your profile doesn't meet all the listed requirements? Don't hesitate to apply anyway! We wouldn't like to miss the opportunity of meeting a great candidate. **About Company** TELUS Digital is the customer experience transformation partner to the world's most admired brands. Our diverse team weaves data, technology, and human ingenuity to deliver differentiated customer journeys, drive operational effectiveness, and scale AI solutions with meaningful value and positive impact. We craft real\-world solutions in the moments that matter, from customer acquisition to lifelong loyalty. Enabled by our global reach \- spanning 78,000 experts in 33 countries \- and deep industry expertise, we help over 600 organizations make the customer experience feel effortless. Our solutions span Data \& AI, Digital Experience \& IT, CX Management and Trust \& Safety. At the core of our innovation is Fuel iX™, an enterprise\-grade generative AI platform that helps clients safely access and optimize leading LLMs to scale their own AI from pilot to production. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** At TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicant's qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: We are currently working with a leading organisation in a regulated manufacturing environment to hire a Plant Engineer for their Dublin site. This is a key hire within the engineering team, where you will play a lead role supporting the Maintenance Manager while taking ownership of site\-wide equipment across process, utilities, and tank farm areas. The position offers strong technical exposure along with the opportunity to drive reliability, improvements, and engineering standards across the site. **The Role:** * Act as the technical expert for your area, ensuring optimal performance of process and utility equipment * Lead maintenance activities across pumps, motors, HVAC, PLC\-controlled systems and instrumentation * Diagnose and resolve mechanical, electrical and automation faults, minimising downtime * Manage and coordinate service providers and contractors on\-site * Support and plan electrical shutdowns in line with safety and operational requirements * Lead root cause analysis on equipment failures and implement corrective actions * Drive continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and reliability * Ensure full compliance with EHS and GMP standards, including accurate documentation **What We Are Looking For:** * 3\+ years’ experience in a plant/equipment engineering role within a regulated environment * Strong exposure to utilities and process systems such as HVAC, purified water, clean steam or refrigeration * Hands\-on experience troubleshooting across mechanical, electrical and automation systems * Experience working in a GMP environment with strong compliance awareness * Engineering qualification (degree or equivalent experience)

title: Maintenance Supervisor
description: **Maintenance Supervisor** Carlton Hotel Blanchardstown \& Carlton Hotel Dublin Airport – Dublin **Why Join Us** * Competitive salary (DOE) * Performance Bonus Scheme (linked to individual and team performance) * Free on\-site parking * Structured, supportive, and performance\-driven environment * Staff meals on duty \& uniform provided * Employee engagement initiatives and social events **About the Role** Carlton Hotels are seeking a Maintenance Supervisor to support the Facilities Manager in overseeing maintenance operations across our hotel group. This is a hands\-on supervisory role responsible for ensuring the effective upkeep, safety, and functionality of all hotel facilities and equipment. You will play a key role in delivering a high\-quality guest experience by maintaining standards across bedrooms, public areas, back\-of\-house, and event spaces. This position contributes directly to: * Guest satisfaction and comfort * Health \& safety compliance * Operational efficiency and cost control * Preventative maintenance and asset longevity You will work closely with all departments, providing technical support while coordinating maintenance activities in a fast\-paced hotel environment. **Performance Bonus Scheme** * Bonus linked to individual performance and hotel results * Clear, measurable KPIs — you know exactly how to earn it * Key focus areas: * Response times \& job completion efficiency * Preventative maintenance delivery * Guest satisfaction (maintenance\-related feedback) * Health \& safety compliance * Cost control and resource management * Direct impact on earnings based on performance and results **Key Responsibilities** * Carry out and supervise day\-to\-day maintenance across hotel facilities (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, general repairs) * Conduct regular inspections to identify maintenance needs and potential risks * Respond promptly to maintenance requests from guests and departments * Support and implement planned preventative maintenance programmes * Maintain accurate records of work orders, inspections, and repairs * Liaise with external contractors and oversee works on\-site * Ensure compliance with all health, safety, fire, and statutory regulations * Monitor stock levels of maintenance supplies and equipment * Assist with event setup and breakdown as required * Support emergency procedures and respond effectively to incidents * Work collaboratively with Front Office, Accommodation, and other departments **The Ideal Candidate** Essential * Minimum 2 years’ experience in maintenance, ideally within a hotel or hospitality environment * Strong technical knowledge across general maintenance (electrical, plumbing, HVAC, etc.) * Proven ability to troubleshoot and resolve maintenance issues efficiently * Good understanding of health \& safety and compliance requirements * Strong organisational and problem\-solving skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Basic computer skills for record\-keeping Desirable * Previous supervisory or team\-leading experience * Experience in a multi\-site or hotel group environment * Knowledge of maintenance management systems **Personal Attributes** * Hands\-on, proactive, and solution\-focused * High attention to detail * Calm and efficient under pressure * Strong work ethic and reliability * Flexible approach to working hours (including weekends if required) * Guest\-focused mindset with minimal disruption approach to works **Location** Carlton Hotel Blanchardstown \& Carlton Hotel Dublin Airport **Job Type** Full\-time, Permanent **Start Date** Immediate Carlton Hotels is an Equal Opportunities Employer. **INDHP** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €32,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Cloghran, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Maintenance: 2 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Marketing Intern
description: **Summer Marketing Internship** – In\-Office (Naas, 3 Days/Week) Location: Naas, Co. Kildare Duration: 3 Months (Summer 2026\) Schedule: 3 Days per Week (Tuesday–Thursday preferred) Are you a student or recent graduate looking to gain hands\-on marketing experience this summer? We're looking for a marketing intern with real design skills and a genuine hunger to learn. You'll work across content creation, visual design, video editing, and campaign support, getting hands\-on experience across everything a marketing agency does. This is a three\-month, unpaid internship, three days a week in our Kildare office. This is a structured learning placement designed to help you build your portfolio, grow your network and develop real\-world marketing skills. **What you'll do** * Assist and create high\-quality visuals for social media, email campaigns, and client materials using Canva * Help develop and execute campaign ideas * Edit short\-form video content using CapCut * Assist with website updates and ongoing design work * Create email marketing newsletters * Support seasonal campaigns and live events * Contribute to content calendars, social planning, and analytics * Support the team with content briefs, asset management, and campaign tracking * Collaborate on brainstorming sessions and bring your ideas to the table. **What we're looking for** * Someone studying or recently graduated in Marketing, Communications, Design, or a related field * Strong verbal and written English. * Must be able to get to the Naas office 3 days per week \- 8\.30 am \-5 pm * Strong Canva skills, you need to be able to design polished, cohesive visuals. This is a must for the role. * A genuine eye for design and visual communication * Confident using social media platforms (especially Instagram, TikTok, and LinkedIn) * A can\-do attitude, willingness to learn and someone who wants to be part of the team * Ability to take feedback, work independently and ask questions when you don't know something * Available for 3 days per week in our Naas office over the summer period * Organised and reliable with deadlines * CapCut experience is a bonus but not essential, we'll teach you if you're keen **What You’ll Gain** * Mentorship and support from an experienced team * Agency insight and client exposure * A reference or completion letter for your CV * What a marketing agency does day to day. **To Apply** Send us your CV to hello@elevatemarketing.ie and a short note about why you're interested, plus any links to past content, design work, or social media examples (optional but encouraged). Job Types: Part\-time, Fixed term Contract length: 3 months Work Location: In person

title: Marketing Technology Analyst
description: No Relocation Assistance Offered Job Number \#172769 \- Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Who We Are** Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name! Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all. **Marketing Technology Analyst, Digital Marketing GIT EMEA** **Help us transform the digital experience for pets and the people who care for them.** We are looking for a high\-energy **Business Analyst** to join our GIT EMEA team. You will **lead** technology enablement for Data Acquisition, Marketing Automation and Omni\-Channel Personalisation for Hill’s EMEA across the pet and vet ecosystem. We aim to create a **seamless, personalized digital experience** that supports veterinarians in their practice and empowers pet parents in their journey. By harmonizing data across every touchpoint, we ensure that every interaction—whether in a clinic or on a smartphone—is relevant, timely, and impactful. **The Mission** 1P CRM data acquisition and activation is a key strategic driver for Hill’s EMEA. You will be the bridge between cutting\-edge technology and real\-world business value, working at the center of the Hill’s EMEA team, as part of the Global IT team, to craft a world\-class digital journey for vets and pet parents. **What You’ll Do** * Master the Data Lifecycle: Maintain a deep, end\-to\-end understanding of data flows across the entire ecosystem. You will ensure that data moves seamlessly between platforms with zero loss, maintaining high integrity from ingestion to activation. * Partner with Global Digital Applications teams to implement MarTech solutions across the entire professional and consumer journey. * Partner with the Hill’s EMEA team to drive acquisition, activation, and harmonization of first\-party data to fuel hyper\-personalized engagement. * Empower Local Markets: You’ll guide local markets on leveraging global platforms while ensuring their specific requirements are translated into technical reality. * Partner with CRM Managers: Work hand\-in\-hand with CRM leads to translate strategic objectives into technical requirements, ensuring data, journeys, and campaigns are built for success. * Be the Tech Ambassador: Translate "tech\-speak" into business value, driving tool adoption and ensuring stakeholders are aligned and inspired. * Safeguard the Experience: Lead regression testing across the ecosystem to ensure technical updates never compromise the user experience. * Document \& Train: Create the "source of truth" documentation and training materials to clarify roles and keep technical knowledge flowing across teams. * Manage the Flow: Navigate agile projects with ease, managing multiple assignments without losing sight of the details. **Who You Are** * Marketing\-Centric: You have a proven track record in Marketing, CRM and Media projects. You understand the "why" behind a campaign and how to speak the language of brand managers. * Technical Specialist: You are hands\-on with data modeling, complex analysis, and activation workflows. You don't just see data; you see the architecture required to move it. * A Solutions Hunter: You don’t just find bugs; you find better ways to work. You are self\-motivated, comfortable with ambiguity, and love to troubleshoot and experiment. * A Master Communicator: You can explain a roadmap to a developer and a business case to a Director with equal clarity. * Technically Sharp: You understand the "how" behind the "what," especially regarding data ingestion and harmonized ecosystems. **Your Toolkit** * Education: Degree in Business, IT, Computer Science, or Engineering. * Tech Literacy: Solid grasp of APIs, data modeling, data analysis, workflow and the digital marketing ecosystem. * Agile Mindset: Proven track record of managing digital initiatives and technology enablement in an agile environment. **Bonus Points For:** * A background in Email Marketing and activation strategy. * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Salesforce Data360, Gigya (Customer Data Cloud), and Snowflake. * Experience with CDP, Adobe Target, AEM, GA4 * Experience with Clean rooms; Infosum, Liveramp, Amazon **Knowledge of SQL, JavaScript, HTML, or CSS.** **Our Commitment to Inclusion** Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business. **Equal Opportunity Employer** Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law. Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Key Responsibilities:** * Ensure technical compliance across all assigned projects * Manage and deliver mechanical design scope * Coordinate consultant selection and define roles/responsibilities (RACI) * Participate in design team meetings * Align design programmes with project planning schedules * Review and coordinate client responsibility matrices with project teams * Manage design processes through RIBA Stages 3–5 * Develop and implement project design management plans * Ensure engineering standards and checklists are applied * Support design for manufacture and assembly where required * Maintain and manage key project documentation and trackers, including procurement schedules, technical submittals, drawings, RFIs, design changes, deviations, and lessons learned * Monitor project progress and carry out regular technical reviews * Support project teams with technical submissions, drawing reviews, and RFIs * Review procurement to ensure cost\-effective solutions * Contribute to value engineering initiatives * Ensure compliance with relevant industry standards (e.g. ISO19650\) * Produce regular project reports * Assist with tendering, estimating, and pre\-construction activities * Support continuous improvement and lessons learned processes * Mentor junior/graduate engineers * Attend site visits as required * Carry out additional design\-related duties as needed **Essential Criteria:** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering or similar * 3\+ years’ experience on large\-scale industrial projects (data centre/pharmaceutical) * Proficiency in mechanical design software * Strong communication skills * High attention to detail and quality of work * Ability to work from a central office location

title: Network Engineer
description: **Location: Dublin, Ireland** **Duration: 12 months** **Engagement Type: Contract\- with opportunities for employment after** **Role Overview** Junior Network Engineer will provide technical support to wider Technology teams, ensuring smooth day\-to\-day network operations. The role focuses on supporting enterprise Aruba\-based network environments, including both wired and wireless infrastructure, with a strong emphasis on ClearPass\-driven access control and segmentation. **Required Technical Skills** **Aruba CX Networking (L2 \& L3\)** Implementation and troubleshooting Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) OSPF routing **Switching \& Stacking Technologies** VSF (Virtual Switching Framework) VSX (Virtual Switching Extension) **Core Networking Concepts** Strong understanding of IPv4 subnetting VLAN segmentation and design principles **Aruba ClearPass** 802\.1X service profiles Integration with Aruba CX 6300 edge switches Dynamic segmentation Deployment of colourless ports **Wireless Networking** Aruba WLAN (AOS8\) deployments Troubleshooting wireless environments **Key Responsibilities** * Network Onboarding \& Configuration * Assist with onboarding of new clients via ClearPass (wired and wireless) * Domain PCs, IoT devices, and medical devices * Support expansion and configuration of Aruba access points * Configure VLANs and IP subnets as directed by Network Design Authority **Troubleshooting \& Support** * Diagnose and resolve wired and wireless connectivity issues * Support IT teams with onboarding of complex devices (e.g. medical/IoT) * Troubleshoot general network issues across LAN and WLAN environments **Operations \& Maintenance** * Perform day\-to\-day network operational tasks, including: * Device backups * Monitoring via IMC (Intelligent Management Center) * Network health checks * Replace faulty hardware (e.g. Aruba CX 6300 VSF switch stacks) **Upgrades \& Improvements** * Plan and implement software upgrades for: * Aruba AOS8 WLAN infrastructure * Wired network environment

title: Network Planning Analyst
description: **Description** Ryanair is currently recruiting for a Network Planning Analyst to join Europe’s biggest airline group. This is an excellent time to join Ryanair as we look to grow to over 800 aircraft and 300m guests per year within the next 10 years. The successful candidate will be based in Ryanair’s Dublin Offices and report directly to the Senior Medium\-Term Planning Manager. You will work as part of a small team responsible for driving Ryanair’s future growth by developing growth strategies for slot constrained airports. **You Will Be Responsible For** * Manage Ryanair’s historic portfolio of high value take\-off / landing slots. * Growing and improving Slot Portfolio’s at strategic airports. * Manage relationships with national slot coordinators, robustly representing Ryanair’s interests externally when required. * Representing Ryanair at international slot conferences * Construction of Ryanair’s schedule * Integration of new aircraft deliveries into the network * Communication of key scheduling information to senior management in a number of departments. * Work closely with other internal departments to ensure the smooth operation of the schedule, announcement of new routes, etc. * Analysis of schedule efficiency. **Requirements** * Highly organised individual with the ability to balance multiple priorities and hit tight deadlines. * Strong attention to detail and a good problem solver. * Good communication skills, both written and verbal. * Strong communication skills * Fluent in English * Third level qualification an advantage * Systems literate **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies Problem Solving/Analysis Adaptability/Flexibility Communication Decision Making/Judgment Job Knowledge Listening Skills Teamwork

title: Node.js Full Stack Developer
description: Node.js Full Stack Developer **Dublin 15 \| Hybrid \| Product Technology Company** Company Snapshot Our client is a highly regarded **product technology company based in Dublin 15** , building sophisticated software platforms used by international customers to improve automation, workflow efficiency, and digital operations at scale. They offer an **excellent salary package, great benefits, and a genuinely nice environment to work in** , backed by a **very strong engineering culture and highly collaborative team setup** . Having worked with this business for a number of years, we can confidently say they have a **very solid team, low attrition, excellent leadership, and strong long term career progression opportunities** . Engineers here are given real ownership, modern tooling, and the opportunity to contribute to meaningful product innovation. What They’re Looking For They are hiring a **Node.js Full Stack Developer** to join a high performing product engineering team focused on building and evolving modern web based software products. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys **owning feature delivery end to end** , from backend services through to polished frontend experiences, while working closely with product, design, and engineering stakeholders. Key experience includes: * 5\+ years of software engineering experience in commercial environments * Strong hands on development with **Node.js, JavaScript, and TypeScript** * Proven backend development experience using **NestJS** * Frontend development expertise with **Vue.js** * Strong experience designing and consuming **RESTful APIs** * Solid understanding of **MySQL, relational databases, and schema design** * Experience working with **AWS cloud services and serverless architecture** * Exposure to **LLM powered tooling, AI agents, or intelligent workflow automation** * Strong focus on **clean code, maintainability, testing, and engineering best practice** * Comfortable working within **Agile product delivery teams** Core Duties The successful developer will play a key role in expanding an established product platform and delivering new capabilities across the stack. Responsibilities will include: * Building new product features across backend services and frontend applications * Developing scalable APIs and services using **Node.js and NestJS** * Creating reusable and responsive interfaces with **Vue.js** * Integrating third party systems and external services * Modernising legacy modules and improving platform performance * Contributing to **AI driven product initiatives and LLM based automation features** * Turning business challenges into scalable technical solutions * Writing robust unit and integration tests using **Jest and TDD methodologies** * Working closely with product and design teams to shape technical solutions * Supporting architectural improvements and continuous product enhancement Nice to Have Additional experience that would be beneficial: * Exposure to **React.js** * Experience in **modern SaaS or product engineering environments** * AWS Lambda or event driven architecture knowledge * CI/CD and DevOps pipeline familiarity * Experience influencing architecture and technical roadmap discussions * Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills * Desire to grow within a **high quality team environment with excellent progression prospects**

title: Operations Associate - Fund Support (Dublin)
description: Operations Associate \- Fund Support (Dublin) **Region** EMEA **Business Unit** Asset Management **Location** Dublin **Job Description** Founded in 1848, Lazard operates in 43 cities across 27 countries with two core businesses: asset management and financial advisory. The Lazard Asset Management Group (‘LAM Group’) has circa €198\.5 billion assets under management across a wide range of global, regional and country\-specific strategies \- both traditional and alternative \- in listed equity and fixed income. Lazard Fund Managers (Ireland) Limited (“LFMI”) is a key part of the LAM Group and a physical office was established in 2014\. LFMI’s main business is the provision of fund management and administration services to collective investment schemes. LFMI manages three UCITS (undertakings for collective investment in transferable securities, which were established in accordance with the EU Directive 2009/65/EC). LFMI and the Funds are regulated by the Central Bank of Ireland (the CBI). LFMI has three branch offices for sales in Belgium, Spain and the Netherlands. At Lazard Asset Management, we pride ourselves in balancing the feel of a small firm with the scale of a large global organization. Our entrepreneurial culture and flat management structure foster an environment in which good ideas can take flight, no matter where they come from. **Operations Associate\- Fund Support \- Dublin** To accompany the development of Lazard Asset Management’s UCITS fund ranges, we are seeking an Operations Associate to join our Fund Support sub team. The Fund Support team, based in Dublin, works closely with the sales and marketing teams to provide fund related material, including PRIIPs KIDs, UCITS KIIDs, factsheets, and data internally and externally. **Responsibilities:** * Co\-ordinate the production of monthly factsheets in multiple languages involving: + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the factsheets from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Uploading data to the document production system and ensuring the data is populated correctly. + Coordinating checks of the factsheets and signing off once validated. + Arranging publication of the factsheets to both LAM website and 3rd party media platforms + Resolving any queries raised in relation to the final published documents. * Co\-ordinate the production of annual and ad\-hoc both PRIIPs KIDs in multiple languages and UCITS KIIDs. + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the PRIIPs KIDs and/or UCITS KIIDs from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Arranging any required translations for PRIIPs KIDs as necessary. + Uploading data to the document production system and ensuring the data is populated correctly. + Coordinating checks of the PRIIPs KIDs/UICTS KIIDs in line with internal procedures, signing off once validated. + Providing the relevant document for filing in the relevant jurisdiction via the internal legal team or external service provider as applicable. + Arranging publication of the documents to LAM website and 3rd party media platforms following receipt of filing confirmation in the relevant jurisdiction. + Resolving any queries raised in relation to the final published documents. * Maintain and manage the relationship with factsheet and KID/KIIDs service * Maintain production logs for the factsheet and KID/ * Liaise with external translators for regular and ad hoc fund documentation * Interface with a range of departments in respect of completion of the documentation, and answer any related queries * Other ad\-hoc duties, g. filing, archiving, sending PDF’s to platforms, answering queries from internal clients * Liaise with subject experts and marketing colleagues across the firm globally to collect and maintain mutual fund relevant information and ensure its accuracy * Ensure data is properly disseminated to data providers and regulators including EMT, EPT, AMT data files. + Sourcing and validating both static and non static data required to populate the data files from both internal and external sources. Any source data issues to be investigated and resolved prior to system upload. + Coordinate production of the file with the relevant service provider and investigate and resolve any data related issues raised during the production process. + Coordinate the dissemination of the data files as necessary and investigate any issues raised by internal or external parties in relation to the files. * Conduct periodic audits of internal and external websites to ensure appropriate documentation is present and updating as required * Maintain appropriate knowledge of the regulations surrounding products in scope * Take a proactive role in launching share classes (obtaining ISINs, tickers, ) * Ensuring the internal product database is up to date at all times to reflect: additional share classes added to Prospectus; newly launched Sub Funds; changes made to existing share classes/Sub Funds. * Complete client templates as required * Any other ad\-hoc tasks as may be required **Essential Skills, Qualifications** * Up to 2 years relevant experience in Fund Administration * We are open to candidates who have relevant internship experience aswell * Comprehensive technical knowledge of UCITS structures across a range of domiciles but predominantly Ireland \& UK * Educated to degree level within a business related discipline **What we offer** We strive to enhance the total health and well\-being of our employees through comprehensive, competitive benefits. Our goal is to offer a highly individualized employee experience that enables you to balance your commitments to career, family, and community. When you work for Lazard, you are working for an organization that cares about your unique talents and passions, and will continue to invest in the development of your career. **Does this sound like you?** Apply! We’ll get in touch on the next steps. **Inclusion at Lazard** Lazard is an intellectual capital business focused on delivering the best advice and solutions to clients. Achieving these objectives requires us to identify, develop and retain the best talent. A workforce comprised of people with varied backgrounds and experiences creates a rich diversity of thought that empowers us to challenge conventional wisdom, as diverse perspectives lead to better decisions. Our appreciation of diversity’s strength is ingrained in our multicultural heritage. As a global firm that has grown organically from local roots in different countries, we have a deep tradition of respect for individual differences, which has been core to our success for 175 years. The ongoing cultivation of an inclusive culture are essential to our continued growth. We are committed to sustaining an environment in which all employees – regardless of socioeconomic status, race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, physical abilities, veteran or military status – can maximize their individual potential, as well as our collective success. \#LI\-EI1 \#LAM

title: Painter & Decorator - Dundalk
description: **Professional Painter \& Decorator** **Location:** Our base is in Dundalk, Co. Louth, where we have two sites, but we also have projects commening in Drogheda and Dunleer **Experience Required:** 5\+ Years as Professional Paintiner \& Decorator with a painting contractor \- No DIY painters please **Employment Type:** Full\-Time (39 hours/week) **About the Role:** We are seeking a skilled and reliable **Painter \& Decorator** with over 5 years of professional experience to join our growing team. title: Part Qualified Tax Associate
description: **About RSM** As one of the world’s largest networks of audit, tax, and consulting firms, RSM is committed to delivering big ideas and premium service to help middle\-market businesses thrive. We are a fast\-growing firm with big ambitions – we have a clear goal to become the premium adviser to the middle market, globally. This vision touches everything we do, motivating and inspiring us to become better every day. If you are looking for a firm where you can build a future and make an impact, then RSM is the place for you. RSM Ireland is one of the country’s fastest growing advisory firms, specialising in the provision of audit, tax and consulting services to leading domestic and international mid\-market clients. We are a key member of the RSM International network and work closely with over 64,000 colleagues in 820 offices, spanning over 120 countries. RSM International is the world’s most rapidly growing accountancy network and currently ranked number six globally. When you join RSM you’ll have a world of opportunity to build the career you want. It’s your future. Own it at RSM. Our clients range from growth\-focused entrepreneurial businesses through to multi\-national organisations across many sectors and operating nationally and across borders. **The Opportunity** RSM have a new and exciting opportunities for a Part Qualified Tax Associate to join our Tax team in a hybrid, work\-from\-home environment. This position offers challenging, interesting, and rewarding work within a global network organisation and would allow the successful candidate to develop core specialisms across a variety of tax sub service lines including Corporation Tax, VAT, Global Employer Services and Transfer Pricing. We will offer full exam support for the successful candidate, including continued in\-house tax technical training and other L\&D supports. **Responsibilities Will Include, But Are Not Limited To** * Working on tax matters with partners and senior management on a portfolio of clients across a range of industries. * Preparing tax returns across all tax heads including income tax, corporation tax and VAT. * Working closing with our tax management team in the delivery of tax consultancy projects across different tax sub service lines. * Developing strong relationships with our clients and international RSM colleagues. **The Person** **Skills** The successful candidate will be a natural self\-starter seeking a fresh challenge with a progressive firm. The ideal candidate will have the following skills and experience: * Part qualified ACA/ACCA/ITI candidate with \+1 years’ experience in a tax role or accounting role involving tax functions. * Excellent technical/analytical and interpersonal skills with excellent attention to detail. * Excellent communication skills. * Flexibility and a team player. * Ability to work on own initiative taking work to completion stage for manager review. *RSM is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Pastry Chef
description: **SCOPE \& GENERAL PURPOSE OF JOB:** To be accountable for pastry production areas of the kitchen, working with the team to ensure that Pastries, Breads and Confectionary are prepared and presented to the agreed highest standards. **ACCOUNTABILITIES:** * To offer the highest level of guest service in the Food Production areas of the hotel, ensuring the 4\* agreed standards are achieved at all times. 2\. To ensure that the standard of products consistently meets the expectation of the customers. 3\. Responsible for the production of fresh breads and pastries in accordance with requests from clients, and to provide same for all food service areas 4\. To prepare and present food to a consistently high standard for all food service areas of the hotel. 5\. To follow all requirements as set down by HACCP, and to supervise junior members of the kitchen team in relation to same 6\. To ensure that all hygiene regulations are adhered to at all times and all food production areas are maintained at a consistently high level of cleanliness. 7\. To ensure that all relevant HACCP documentation is completed throughout the working day 8\. To document and report back to supervisors and management any non\-compliance in relation to HACCP control within the kitchen 9\. To comply with statutory and legal requirements for Health \& Safety, Fire, Hygiene, Licensing and Employment. 10\. To have pride and commitment in your area of work. 11\. To attend training sessions as required. 12\. To attend appraisal / assess as required. 13\. To attend Hotel and Departmental Meetings as required, ensuring effective communication at all levels. 14\. To maintain all company policies and procedures. **This is not a full and final list of duties and responsibilities and you may be required to carry out other tasks as directed by senior executives.**

title: People Associate
description: At Flipdish, we’re turning the tables in favour of independent restaurant and takeaway owners by providing them with the tools they need to make their lives easier. We offer a suite of powerful yet straightforward tech solutions along with real human support, helping our customers thrive on their own terms. Everything we do is designed to make running a restaurant satisfyingly simple in a world that’s anything but. **About the role** We’re looking for a People Associate to join our People team. This is a hands\-on role with exposure across the full employee life cycle, working closely with a small but mighty People team. You will support day\-to\-day People operations while also getting involved in broader People initiatives, projects and improvements. This role is ideal for someone early in their People career who wants to make an impact across a growing scale\-up, take on real responsibility, and loves variety **What you will be doing** * Supporting the full employee life cycle from onboarding to offboarding * Managing People Operations tasks including contracts, documentation, system updates and employee records * Coordinating onboarding and induction for new hires * Supporting performance review cycles and career development processes * Assisting with employee relations queries and escalating where appropriate * Maintaining accuracy across People systems and reports * Supporting compliance and policy administration * Getting involved in wider People initiatives such as engagement, wellbeing, learning and development or process improvements * Working closely with managers and other teams to get things done efficiently **What we are looking for** * Early experience in a People, HR or People Operations role, or a strong interest in building a career in this area * Excellent attention to detail and a high standard of accuracy * Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks at once * A proactive, hands\-on approach with a focus on getting things done * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, scaling environment * Strong communication skills and a professional, people\-first approach * Discretion and the ability to handle confidential information appropriately **What you will get** * Broad exposure across People Operations in a scaling SaaS environment * Hands\-on experience rather than narrow admin\-only work * Support, mentoring and a clear development pathway within the People function * The opportunity to make an impact and help shape how we work as a company 🦄 **About Flipdish** We are Flipdish, an Irish unicorn valued at over $1bn, helping independent restaurants, takeaways and quick service brands compete and grow in a world transformed by technology. Our platform has evolved into a fully integrated, AI powered ecosystem that supports restaurants with online ordering, marketing, loyalty, point of sale, menu optimisation and operational automation. AI is at the heart of many of our newest innovations, including the Flipdish AI Phone Agent, which answers customer calls, takes orders and ensures no order is ever missed. From AI driven marketing to intelligent menu tools, we build technology that makes running a restaurant satisfyingly simple in a world that is anything but. But Flipdish is not just about technology. We are deeply connected to the industry we serve. Many of us have worked in restaurants, run franchises or grown food businesses ourselves. We understand the pressures operators face, and we support them with the same intensity and care we would want for our own businesses. **🫀Our culture** At this point, you might expect the usual spiel about how great a place Flipdish is to work at and how great our work culture is (and it *is* !). But, this all depends on our employees. And that’s because we trust our employees to define what sort of an employer Flipdish is. Transparency is hugely important to us. We listen to one another. We collaborate. And we give everyone a way to grow their careers through a supportive environment. We’re a company that values diversity, inclusion, and wellbeing as standard. We believe in balance, not burnout. And all permanent employees qualify for our share scheme because we share our success. We certainly encourage flexibility where possible and combine this with beautiful offices in bustling cities like Dublin, London and Karachi. We’re focused on getting the most out of our time together, output and team energy is at the heart of our hub based philosophy \- not an attendance ticking exercise! So, if you want to join a genuinely friendly team, take a look at our current openings and get in touch. And if you don’t see an opening just yet, do get in touch and register your interest with us anyway. We’ll be in touch as soon as something suitable opens up. **🙈Don’t be shy. Apply.** We know that imposter syndrome can be a thing. Don’t let it be. We want enthusiastic people from all backgrounds. If you feel like you’re up for working with us, get in touch. We also understand some candidates might need assistance. That’s not a problem in the slightest. Just reach out to us, we’d love to hear from you.
